texto: Even the water, grey and listless as it tossed against the harbour wall, seemed fixed in time; as if peering hard enough into its depths would reveal the tips of Peter’s fingers, himself still swaying underwater, cradled in the sea’s mouth.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ava Bloomfield: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Even the water, grey and listless as it tossed against the harbour wall, seemed fixed in time; as…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, death, perception pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e perception.
